extraction.one_line_summary: WOUT VAN AERT launched latest from chaos sprint in Albi, beating VIVIANI after INEOS/ALAPHILIPPE created echelon carnage that dropped PINOT/URAN/PORTE/FUGLSANG 2+ minutes.
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE/INEOS weaponized crosswinds pre-sprint to eliminate GC threats PINOT/URAN/PORTE/FUGLSANG, gaining 2+ minutes on flat sprint stage.
VIVIANI pulling through early exposed INEOS intent to destroy field, not just position sprinter—echelon tactic disguised as leadout.
VAN AERT launching last from chaotic position won over early launchers MATTHEWS/SAGAN—late acceleration beats positioning on disorganized sprints.
MITCHELTON sacrificed sprint result putting YATES safely positioned during echelon, revealing GC protection trumps stage hunting even for non-contenders.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: yellow jersey actively driving echelon destruction on sprint stage shows race leadership mentality, not passive defense
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: working front during echelon proves INEOS uses sprinter as diesel engine for tactical warfare, not just finisher
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: caught out losing 2+ minutes in crosswinds on routine sprint stage exposes positioning/team weakness for GC threat
rider_signals.URAN Rigoberto: negative: 2+ minute loss in echelon shows EF positioning failures, likely eliminating podium contention by stage 10
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: dropped in crosswinds echelon reveals Trek team weakness protecting GC rider in tactical situations
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: negative: losing 2+ minutes in sprint stage echelon questions Astana's ability to protect their leader in tactical racing
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: MITCHELTON prioritized his safety over MATTHEWS sprint, indicates genuine GC commitment despite ranking
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: fourth stage win from launching latest in messy sprint shows acceleration advantage beats positioning chaos
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: perfect position again without converting reveals finishing deficiency, not positioning problem
